# Load candidate symbols from stockinfo result

with open(path2, 'r') as f:
    tables = json.load(f)

available_tables = set(tables)

valid_symbols = list(candidate_symbols.intersection(available_tables))

sq = chr(39) # single quote
dq = chr(34) # double quote

queries = []
for sym in valid_symbols:
    # SELECT 'SYM' AS Symbol, SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS UpDays, SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DownDays FROM "SYM" WHERE "Date" >= '2017-01-01' AND "Date" <= '2017-12-31'
    q = "SELECT " + sq + sym + sq + " AS Symbol, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " > " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS UpDays, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " < " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DownDays " + \
        "FROM " + dq + sym + dq + " " + \
        "WHERE " + dq + "Date" + dq + " >= " + sq + "2017-01-01" + sq + " AND " + dq + "Date" + dq + " <= " + sq + "2017-12-31" + sq
    queries.append(q)

full_query = " UNION ALL ".join(queries)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(full_query))

path1 = locals()['var_function-call-6393703521614786747']
with open(path1, 'r') as f:
    candidates = json.load(f)

candidate_symbols = set(c['Symbol'] for c in candidates)

path2 = locals()['var_function-call-14160190749123252694']
with open(path2, 'r') as f:
    tables = json.load(f)

available_tables = set(tables)

valid_symbols = sorted(list(candidate_symbols.intersection(available_tables)))

print(len(valid_symbols))

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(valid_symbols))

args.code:
import json

symbols = ["AEFC", "AIN", "AIV", "AIZP", "AJRD", "AL", "AMN", "AMP", "AMT", "ARD", "ARGD", "ARLO", "ASG", "AVA", "BANC", "BBU", "BBVA", "BDXA", "BKH", "BKT", "BLD", "BNS", "BV", "BZH", "CADE", "CAE", "CAF", "CBT", "CCC", "CCZ", "CHAP", "CIA", "CMA", "CMI", "CMSA", "CNK", "COTY", "CRC", "CRM", "CRS", "CSL", "CTS", "CUBE", "CURO", "CVIA", "CVX", "CXH", "DAC", "DDS", "DDT", "DEO", "DGX", "DMB", "DTQ", "DXC", "EARN", "EBS", "EGO", "EGY", "EIG", "ELF", "EMP", "ENLC", "EPR", "EPRT", "ES", "ESRT", "ESS", "ETM", "EV", "EVT", "EXP", "FMN", "FPAC", "FSM", "GCO", "GD", "GDL", "GDV", "GEL", "GJP", "GLOB", "GLT", "GOL", "GSLD", "GTY", "GVA", "GWB", "H", "HBI", "HDB", "HEP", "HIL", "HIO", "HIX", "HLF", "HLT", "HNI", "HRB", "HTFA", "IBM", "IGR", "IHC", "IPG", "IRM", "IT", "JGH", "JHY", "JKS", "JMP", "JNPR", "KMB", "KNX", "KW", "KYN", "LB", "LDOS", "LHC"]
# 115 symbols

sq = chr(39)
dq = chr(34)

queries = []
for sym in symbols:
    q = "SELECT " + sq + sym + sq + " AS Symbol, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " > " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS UpDays, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " < " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DownDays " + \
        "FROM " + dq + sym + dq + " " + \
        "WHERE " + dq + "Date" + dq + " >= " + sq + "2017-01-01" + sq + " AND " + dq + "Date" + dq + " <= " + sq + "2017-12-31" + sq
    queries.append(q)

full_query = " UNION ALL ".join(queries)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(full_query))

args.code:
import json

# Valid symbols list from previous step
symbols = ["AEFC", "AIN", "AIV", "AIZP", "AJRD", "AL", "AMN", "AMP", "AMT", "ARD", "ARGD", "ARLO", "ASG", "AVA", "BANC", "BBU", "BBVA", "BDXA", "BKH", "BKT", "BLD", "BNS", "BV", "BZH", "CADE", "CAE", "CAF", "CBT", "CCC", "CCZ", "CHAP", "CIA", "CMA", "CMI", "CMSA", "CNK", "COTY", "CRC", "CRM", "CRS"] 
# 40 symbols

sq = chr(39)
dq = chr(34)

queries = []
for sym in symbols:
    q = "SELECT " + sq + sym + sq + " AS Symbol, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " > " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS UpDays, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " < " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DownDays " + \
        "FROM " + dq + sym + dq + " " + \
        "WHERE " + dq + "Date" + dq + " >= " + sq + "2017-01-01" + sq + " AND " + dq + "Date" + dq + " <= " + sq + "2017-12-31" + sq
    queries.append(q)

full_query = " UNION ALL ".join(queries)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(full_query))

import json

# Valid symbols list from previous step
symbols = ["AEFC", "AIN", "AIV", "AIZP", "AJRD", "AL", "AMN", "AMP", "AMT", "ARD", "ARGD", "ARLO", "ASG", "AVA", "BANC", "BBU", "BBVA", "BDXA", "BKH", "BKT", "BLD", "BNS", "BV", "BZH", "CADE", "CAE", "CAF", "CBT", "CCC", "CCZ", "CHAP", "CIA", "CMA", "CMI", "CMSA", "CNK", "COTY", "CRC", "CRM", "CRS", "CSL", "CTS", "CUBE", "CURO", "CVIA", "CVX", "CXH", "DAC", "DDS", "DDT", "DEO", "DGX", "DMB", "DTQ", "DXC", "EARN", "EBS", "EGO", "EGY", "EIG", "ELF", "EMP", "ENLC", "EPR", "EPRT", "ES", "ESRT", "ESS", "ETM", "EV", "EVT", "EXP", "FMN", "FPAC", "FSM", "GCO", "GD", "GDL", "GDV", "GEL", "GJP", "GLOB", "GLT", "GOL", "GSLD", "GTY", "GVA", "GWB", "H", "HBI", "HDB", "HEP", "HIL", "HIO", "HIX", "HLF", "HLT", "HNI", "HRB", "HTFA", "IBM", "IGR", "IHC", "IPG", "IRM", "IT", "JGH", "JHY", "JKS", "JMP", "JNPR", "KMB", "KNX", "KW", "KYN", "LB", "LDOS", "LHC", "LHX", "LOMA", "MANU", "MDLX", "MDLY", "MED", "MFO", "MGR", "MGU", "MHE", "MIY", "MKC", "MLI", "MNE", "MR", "MS", "MTD", "MYD", "NFH", "NGG", "NJV", "NNI", "NNY", "NRUC", "NUE", "NXN", "OCFT", "OEC", "ORA", "ORAN", "ORCL", "ORN", "PAG", "PBI", "PFE", "PFSI", "PGR", "PIM", "PKE", "PLAN", "PLNT", "PMT", "PNM", "PPG", "PRSP", "PRTY", "PSV", "PSXP", "QTS", "QUAD", "RBC", "RCB", "RCI", "RES", "REXR", "RH", "RMT", "ROG", "ROL", "RPAI", "RPM", "RQI", "RWT", "SAF", "SAIL", "SAM", "SBR", "SCU", "SFUN", "SHAK", "SI", "SITC", "SJM", "SJT", "SJW", "SLF", "SMP", "SOL", "SPOT", "SRC", "SRF", "SRT", "SSD", "STG", "STL", "STON", "SYX", "TBB", "TCP", "TDJ", "TGP", "TLYS", "TNC", "TPH", "TRV", "TTI", "TUFN", "TWTR", "UHT", "UIS", "USX", "UTL", "VET", "VGR", "VHI", "VIV", "VKQ", "VRT", "VVI", "WOR", "WPG", "WSM", "X", "YEXT", "ZNH", "ZTR"]

batch2 = symbols[40:100] # 60 symbols

sq = chr(39)
dq = chr(34)

queries = []
for sym in batch2:
    q = "SELECT " + sq + sym + sq + " AS Symbol, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " > " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS UpDays, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " < " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DownDays " + \
        "FROM " + dq + sym + dq + " " + \
        "WHERE " + dq + "Date" + dq + " >= " + sq + "2017-01-01" + sq + " AND " + dq + "Date" + dq + " <= " + sq + "2017-12-31" + sq
    queries.append(q)

full_query = " UNION ALL ".join(queries)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(full_query))

import json

# Valid symbols list from previous step
symbols = ["AEFC", "AIN", "AIV", "AIZP", "AJRD", "AL", "AMN", "AMP", "AMT", "ARD", "ARGD", "ARLO", "ASG", "AVA", "BANC", "BBU", "BBVA", "BDXA", "BKH", "BKT", "BLD", "BNS", "BV", "BZH", "CADE", "CAE", "CAF", "CBT", "CCC", "CCZ", "CHAP", "CIA", "CMA", "CMI", "CMSA", "CNK", "COTY", "CRC", "CRM", "CRS", "CSL", "CTS", "CUBE", "CURO", "CVIA", "CVX", "CXH", "DAC", "DDS", "DDT", "DEO", "DGX", "DMB", "DTQ", "DXC", "EARN", "EBS", "EGO", "EGY", "EIG", "ELF", "EMP", "ENLC", "EPR", "EPRT", "ES", "ESRT", "ESS", "ETM", "EV", "EVT", "EXP", "FMN", "FPAC", "FSM", "GCO", "GD", "GDL", "GDV", "GEL", "GJP", "GLOB", "GLT", "GOL", "GSLD", "GTY", "GVA", "GWB", "H", "HBI", "HDB", "HEP", "HIL", "HIO", "HIX", "HLF", "HLT", "HNI", "HRB", "HTFA", "IBM", "IGR", "IHC", "IPG", "IRM", "IT", "JGH", "JHY", "JKS", "JMP", "JNPR", "KMB", "KNX", "KW", "KYN", "LB", "LDOS", "LHC", "LHX", "LOMA", "MANU", "MDLX", "MDLY", "MED", "MFO", "MGR", "MGU", "MHE", "MIY", "MKC", "MLI", "MNE", "MR", "MS", "MTD", "MYD", "NFH", "NGG", "NJV", "NNI", "NNY", "NRUC", "NUE", "NXN", "OCFT", "OEC", "ORA", "ORAN", "ORCL", "ORN", "PAG", "PBI", "PFE", "PFSI", "PGR", "PIM", "PKE", "PLAN", "PLNT", "PMT", "PNM", "PPG", "PRSP", "PRTY", "PSV", "PSXP", "QTS", "QUAD", "RBC", "RCB", "RCI", "RES", "REXR", "RH", "RMT", "ROG", "ROL", "RPAI", "RPM", "RQI", "RWT", "SAF", "SAIL", "SAM", "SBR", "SCU", "SFUN", "SHAK", "SI", "SITC", "SJM", "SJT", "SJW", "SLF", "SMP", "SOL", "SPOT", "SRC", "SRF", "SRT", "SSD", "STG", "STL", "STON", "SYX", "TBB", "TCP", "TDJ", "TGP", "TLYS", "TNC", "TPH", "TRV", "TTI", "TUFN", "TWTR", "UHT", "UIS", "USX", "UTL", "VET", "VGR", "VHI", "VIV", "VKQ", "VRT", "VVI", "WOR", "WPG", "WSM", "X", "YEXT", "ZNH", "ZTR"]

batch2 = symbols[40:100] # 60 symbols

sq = chr(39)
dq = chr(34)

queries = []
for sym in batch2:
    q = "SELECT " + sq + sym + sq + " AS Symbol, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " > " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS UpDays, " + \
        "SUM(CASE WHEN " + dq + "Close" + dq + " < " + dq + "Open" + dq + " THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DownDays " + \
        "FROM " + dq + sym + dq + " " + \
        "WHERE " + dq + "Date" + dq + " >= " + sq + "2017-01-01" + sq + " AND " + dq + "Date" + dq + " <= " + sq + "2017-12-31" + sq
    queries.append(q)

full_query = " UNION ALL ".join(queries)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(full_query))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Load stock info
with open(locals()['var_function-call-6393703521614786747'], 'r') as f:
    stock_info = json.load(f)

# Create map Symbol -> Name
# Extract name from Company Description
# Heuristic: Take string before " is ", " specializes ", " operates ", " provides ", " offers ", " engages ".
# If not found, take first 5 words?
def extract_name(desc):
    if not desc: return "Unknown"
    # Common splitters
    splitters = [" is ", " specializes ", " operates ", " provides ", " offers ", " engages ", " focuses ", " aims ", " excelling ", " excels ", " committed ", " founded ", " known ", " headquartered ", " mainly ", " primarily "]
    min_idx = len(desc)
    used_splitter = None
    for s in splitters:
        idx = desc.find(s)
        if idx != -1 and idx < min_idx:
            min_idx = idx
            used_splitter = s
    
    if min_idx < len(desc):
        name = desc[:min_idx]
    else:
        # Fallback: take first 4-5 words
        name = " ".join(desc.split()[:5])
    
    return name.strip()

symbol_to_name = {x['Symbol']: extract_name(x['Company Description']) for x in stock_info}

# Load batch results
files = [
    locals()['var_function-call-12464845564042619794'],

import json
import pandas as pd

# Load stock info
with open(locals()['var_function-call-6393703521614786747'], 'r') as f:
    stock_info = json.load(f)

# Create map Symbol -> Name
# Extract name from Company Description
# Heuristic: Take string before " is ", " specializes ", " operates ", " provides ", " offers ", " engages ".
# If not found, take first 5 words?
def extract_name(desc):
    if not desc: return "Unknown"
    # Common splitters
    splitters = [" is ", " specializes ", " operates ", " provides ", " offers ", " engages ", " focuses ", " aims ", " excelling ", " excels ", " committed ", " founded ", " known ", " headquartered ", " mainly ", " primarily "]
    min_idx = len(desc)
    used_splitter = None
    for s in splitters:
        idx = desc.find(s)
        if idx != -1 and idx < min_idx:
            min_idx = idx
            used_splitter = s
    
    if min_idx < len(desc):
        name = desc[:min_idx]
    else:
        # Fallback: take first 4-5 words
        name = " ".join(desc.split()[:5])
    
    return name.strip()

symbol_to_name = {x['Symbol']: extract_name(x['Company Description']) for x in stock_info}

# Load batch results
files = [
    locals()['var_function-call-12464845564042619794'],
    locals()['var_function-call-17259338578675585453'],
    locals()['var_function-call-5663784839443484320'],
    locals()['var_function-call-8225100761443059224']
]

results = []
for file_path in files:
    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
        # Check if file contains the list or if I need to parse it
        # The tool result is a list of dicts.
        data = json.load(f)
        results.extend(data)

df = pd.DataFrame(results)

# Clean numeric columns
df['UpDays'] = pd.to_numeric(df['UpDays'], errors='coerce')
df['DownDays'] = pd.to_numeric(df['DownDays'], errors='coerce')

# Drop NaNs
df = df.dropna(subset=['UpDays', 'DownDays'])

# Filter: UpDays > DownDays
df_filtered = df[df['UpDays'] > df['DownDays']].copy()

# Calculate Net Up Days
df_filtered['NetUp'] = df_filtered['UpDays'] - df_filtered['DownDays']

# Sort
# I will output Top 10 by UpDays to see.
top_by_up = df_filtered.sort_values(by='UpDays', ascending=False).head(10)
top_by_net = df_filtered.sort_values(by='NetUp', ascending=False).head(10)

# Add Names
top_by_up['Name'] = top_by_up['Symbol'].map(symbol_to_name)
top_by_net['Name'] = top_by_net['Symbol'].map(symbol_to_name)

print("Top by Up Days:")
print(top_by_up[['Symbol', 'Name', 'UpDays', 'DownDays']].to_string())
print("\nTop by Net Up Days:")
print(top_by_net[['Symbol', 'Name', 'UpDays', 'DownDays', 'NetUp']].to_string())

# Prepare Result for JSON
# I'll return the list of names for the top 5 by Up Days
final_list = top_by_up.head(5)[['Name', 'Symbol']].to_dict(orient='records')

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(final_list))

import json
import pandas as pd

p1 = locals()['var_function-call-6393703521614786747']
with open(p1, 'r') as f:
    stock_info = json.load(f)

def get_name(d):
    if not d: return "Unknown"
    # simplify splitter
    s = d.split(" is ")[0]
    if len(s) < len(d): return s.strip()
    s = d.split(" specializes ")[0]
    if len(s) < len(d): return s.strip()
    return " ".join(d.split()[:5]).strip()

sym_map = {x['Symbol']: get_name(x['Company Description']) for x in stock_info}

files = [
    locals()['var_function-call-12464845564042619794'],
    locals()['var_function-call-17259338578675585453'],
    locals()['var_function-call-5663784839443484320'],
    locals()['var_function-call-8225100761443059224']
]

data = []
for fp in files:
    with open(fp, 'r') as f:
        data.extend(json.load(f))

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df['UpDays'] = pd.to_numeric(df['UpDays'], errors='coerce')
df['DownDays'] = pd.to_numeric(df['DownDays'], errors='coerce')
df = df.dropna()

df = df[df['UpDays'] > df['DownDays']]
top = df.sort_values('UpDays', ascending=False).head(5)

res = []
for _, row in top.iterrows():

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(res))

    s = d.split(" is ")[0]
    if len(s) < len(d): return s.strip()

    if len(s) < len(d): return s.strip()
    return " ".join(d.split()[:5]).strip()

        data.extend(val)

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
